pass1_value.note: User-facing integrations use OAuth for connecting third-party sources. No public API authentication docs or API reference discovered on official docs; therefore 'none_public' indicates no public developer API found.

final_value.note: Waterfall states it uses OAuth 2.0 (where available) to request permissioned access when connecting third-party services, and it does not store third-party account passwords.

pass1_value.note: Pricing page shows Free/Pro/Business tiers; no documentation on obtaining API credentials or whether API access is available on plans. Contact/sales page suggests enterprise contact for custom solutions.

final_value.note: The pricing page FAQ explicitly says: "Do you have an API? No, Waterfall does not currently offer a public API." Therefore there is no self-serve way to obtain API credentials.

pass1_value.note: No public REST/GraphQL API docs or SDKs found on official domains; only user integration articles. 'narrow' used because only connectors are documented (OAuth integrations), not a public API surface.

final_value.note: Waterfall's pricing FAQ states it does not currently offer a public API; the product instead provides access/export via dashboards and reports.

pass1_value.note: Cannot build an AI-agent integration today because there is no public developer API documentation or a documented way to self-serve API credentials. Recommend contacting Waterfall sales via https://waterfall.io/contact/ to inquire about partner/enterprise API access.

final_value.note: Waterfall states it does not currently offer a public API (pricing FAQ). Without a public API and self-serve credentials, an AI-agent integration cannot be built via standard API methods.
